articles.title: 400+ Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Install Rust Credential Stealer
articles.summary: Attackers took over more than 400 packages in the Arch User Repository (AUR) this week and rewrote their build scripts to install a credential stealer on any machine that built them. The malware is a Rust binary built to harvest developer secrets. When it lands with root, it can also load an eBPF rootkit to hide itself. The AUR is Arch Linux's community package collection, and it is separate

keypoints: 400+ AUR packages compromised with credential-stealing malware
Rust stealer loads eBPF rootkit for persistence

analysis.title: Supply chain attack scope
analysis.desc: Attackers hijacked 400+ community packages simultaneously, injecting malware into build scripts targeting developers.

analysis.title: Malware capabilities
analysis.desc: Credential stealer harvests developer secrets; escalates to root for eBPF rootkit installation and evasion.

analysis.title: Trust infrastructure risk
analysis.desc: AUR's community-maintained model lacks centralized security review, enabling mass compromise of build pipelines.

ai_summary.suggested_title: AUR Supply Chain Attack: 400+ Packages Infected Credential Stealer
ai_summary.bottom_line: If you use AUR packages, your API keys and secrets are already exposed.
ai_summary.action_today: Arch Linux → pacman -Sy && pacman -Qu | audit recent builds (now)

articles.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: Worm Code Leaked, AI Agent Phished, Claude Code Patch + 28 New Stories
articles.summary: It's been one of those weeks. You expect the usual noise: recycled malware, sloppy attacks, another easy target getting hit. Instead, there's a supply chain attack kit in a public repo, a $5,000-a-month RAT that clones browsers, and research showing AI agents can be tricked into leaking real credentials. The bigger problem is how polished this all looks now. Mule networks run like SaaS.

keypoints: Supply chain attack kits publicly exposed in repositories
AI agents manipulated into revealing legitimate user credentials

analysis.title: Polished criminal infrastructure
analysis.desc: Mule networks and RAT services operate with SaaS-level professionalism, lowering barriers for attackers.

analysis.title: AI agent vulnerability exploitation
analysis.desc: Researchers demonstrate AI systems can be socially engineered to leak real credentials through phishing.

analysis.title: Democratized attack tooling
analysis.desc: Leaked supply chain attack kits in public repos enable less-skilled threat actors to execute sophisticated campaigns.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Supply Chain Attacks, AI Phishing, Claude Patch — 28 New Threats
ai_summary.bottom_line: Your AI assistant just leaked your credentials to someone pretending to be support.
ai_summary.action_today: Audit → Passwords → Check if any accounts use same password as email (lastpass.com or 1password.com)

articles.title: Coupang hit with record $409 million data breach fine in Korea
articles.summary: ​​The Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC), South Korea's data protection regulator, has fined e-commerce giant Coupang a record 624.6 billion won (roughly $409 million) following a massive data breach affecting more than 37 million customers [...]

keypoints: South Korea fines Coupang record $409M for massive breach
37 million customers affected by e-commerce platform data exposure

analysis.title: Record penalty signals enforcement
analysis.desc: PIPC's largest fine demonstrates aggressive regulatory response to corporate data mishandling and negligence.

analysis.title: Scale of exposure staggering
analysis.desc: 37M customer records compromised represents significant portion of Korean population, indicating systemic security failures.

analysis.title: Accountability gap widening
analysis.desc: Major platforms still prioritize growth over security despite increasing fines, leaving users persistently vulnerable.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Data Breach: Coupang Hit with Record $409M Fine
ai_summary.bottom_line: If you shopped at Coupang, assume your personal data is already in criminal hands.
ai_summary.action_today: Check your email for breach notifications from Coupang, change your password immediately if affected
